questions.question: After running `score = 10`, what does the name `score` refer to?

options.explanation: Right. `=` makes the name on the left point to the value on the right, so `score` now refers to 10.
The name is just a label; it doesn't store its own letters as a value.
The variable holds 10 immediately after the assignment — printing only displays it, it isn't required to create it.

questions.question: Which of these is a string (text), not a number?

options.explanation: With no quotes, `42` is an integer — a number you can do arithmetic with.
Correct. The quotes tell Python this is text. `"42"` is the characters 4 and 2, not the number 42.
`3.14` has no quotes and a decimal point, so it's a float (a decimal number).
